channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ghana High Commission Advises Nationals to Exercise Caution Amid Johannesburg Protest
item.description: The Ghana High Commission in South Africa has issued an official safety advisory for Ghanaian nationals following a protest in the Johannesburg Central Business District (CBD) on 29 April. The protest...

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship near Cape Verde raises health concerns but low public risk
item.description: One cruise ship carrying 149 people is currently stranded near Cape Verde after a hantavirus outbreak led to the death of three passengers and left others seriously ill. Authorities refused the ship p...

item.title: Woman killed by partner before gas explosion at their London home
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Stoke Newington, east London, in June 2025, when 46-year-old Annabel Rook was fatally attacked by her partner, 45-year-old Clifton George. According to court reports, the...

item.title: Amazon Launches Contest for U.S. Residents to Submit Alien or UFO Footage
item.description: Amazon-owned Ring has announced a contest inviting U.S. residents to capture evidence of extraterrestrial life using their Ring devices. Participants can submit videos featuring aliens or UFOs, but en...

item.title: Del Monte Kenya’s Economic Impact Surpasses Sh100 Billion Over Two Decades
item.description: Del Monte Kenya has made a significant contribution to the country’s economy, exceeding Sh100 billion over a 20-year period between 2004 and 2024. According to an impact report prepared by Lotus Consu...

item.title: PTI confirms Punjab Coordination Committee decision remains unchanged
item.description: Islamabad – PTI Secretary General Salman Akram Raja on Wednesday clarified that the party would not discuss the formation of the Punjab Coordination and Monitoring Committee at the moment, as leadersh...

item.title: Robert Irwin recalls spilling orange juice during lunch with King Charles
item.description: Robert Irwin, the young Australian wildlife conservationist and son of the late Steve Irwin, shared a humorous yet mortifying experience from his youth while appearing on The Tonight Show with Jimmy F...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Treatment of Jewish Candidate in Ontario Medical Association Election
item.description: Hal Berman, a Jewish palliative care physician in Toronto, alleges that he was singled out during the Ontario Medical Association (OMA) presidential election due to his social media posts addressing a...

item.title: Notable Australian Book Releases for May 2026
item.description: May 2026 brings an exciting array of Australian books spanning fiction, nonfiction, memoir, and cookery. Amanda Lohrey’s new novel, 'Capture', explores the world of alien abduction through a psychiatr...

item.title: Jacqueline's unwavering support for Vincent in key scenes of 'The Alibi'
item.description: In the popular TV series 'The Alibi,' Jacqueline Cabrera consistently demonstrates her loyalty and devotion to Vincent, played by Paulo Avelino, throughout several pivotal moments. From emotional conf...

item.title: Aisha Yesufu resigns from ADC, joins NDC to contest FCT Senatorial seat
item.description: Political and civil rights activist Aisha Yesufu don officially resign from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) and join the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC). She announce this matter on her X page...

item.title: Ukrainian Artist Visualizes the Collapse of Russia Through Passport Covers
item.description: Frustrated by the constant stream of disturbing news, Ukrainian artist Anton Karyuk began his project 'The Empire Has Collapsed.' This ongoing series of passport cover designs imagines a world where a...

item.title: Severe Weather Causes Fatalities and Property Damage Across South Africa
item.description: Severe weather across South Africa has resulted in tragic fatalities and significant property damage. In Ekurhuleni, Gauteng, a 20-year-old woman named Seluleko was killed when a tree fell on the shac...

item.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87, Leaves Lasting Legacy in Media and Philanthropy
item.description: Ted Turner, the legendary media entrepreneur and founder of CNN, has passed away at the age of 87. Widely recognised for introducing the 24-hour news cycle, Turner transformed global media and built a...

item.title: Exploring Mumbai’s Culinary Diversity Through Its Unique Foods
item.description: Mumbai’s culinary scene is a fascinating mix of history, culture, and innovation, as explored by Pronoti Datta in her new book, 'In the Beginning There Was Bombay Duck'. The city offers a spectrum of ...

item.title: Ukraine Forms Working Group to Decide the Fate of the Friendship of Peoples Arch in Kyiv
item.description: The Ukrainian Cabinet of Ministers has tasked the Ministry of Culture and Information Policy, along with the Kyiv City State Administration, to determine the future use of the People's Friendship Arch...

item.title: Detroit to Join PWHL as Ninth Team for Upcoming Season
item.description: The Premier Women's Hockey League (PWHL) has announced Detroit as the location for its ninth team, set to start competing next season. This expansion marks the league’s third new team since it began i...

item.title: Digitizing the Photographic Legacy of Vitaliy Kuzovkov: Kyiv in the 1980s
item.description: Darka Simchich, a 23-year-old educational manager at Kyiv-Mohyla Business School, recently began digitizing the film archive of her great-grandfather, photographer Vitaliy Kuzovkov. His vibrant and wa...

item.title: Mike Cannon-Brookes leads Atlassian solo amid AI-driven product expansion and workforce changes
item.description: Mike Cannon-Brookes, co-founder of Atlassian, is navigating the company solo for nearly two years after Scott Farquhar stepped back. The Sydney-based software giant recently faced a 70% drop in share ...

item.title: Boy in Odisha Trapped in Crevice Encounters Snake
item.description: In a startling incident from Odisha, a young boy got his head stuck in a narrow crevice while playing near his home. As he struggled to free himself, he noticed a snake staring directly at him, which ...
